args.message: Great question — shows you know what to look for. This Tahoe came from a single-owner fleet lease return, so it's had really consistent, professional care throughout its life. args.message: Can you provide specifics on the maintenance records? For instance, how often were the oil changes performed, and were there any major repairs or issues documented during its service history?

text: Can you provide specifics on the maintenance records? For instance, how often were the oil changes performed, and were there any major repairs or issues documented during its service history?

speaker: seller

args.message: Absolutely — I've got the records right here. Oil changes were done every 5,000 to 7,500 miles, which is textbook Chevy protocol for the 5.3L. No major repairs on file — this thing has been bulletproof. Normal wear items like brake pads and filters, but nothing structural or engine-related. The transmission has been smooth as silk, and we just had our tech do a full once-over last week — fluids all look good, no codes thrown. This Tahoe is genuinely low-maintenance. That's the beauty of the 5.3L platform — it's one of the most reliable V8s GM ever built.

speaker: seller

args.message: Good follow-up. By normal wear items, I'm talking about consumables — brake pads, air filters, cabin filters, that kind of thing. Nothing surprising for a vehicle at this mileage. Transmission and suspension have both been rock solid. No leaks, no shifting issues, no strange noises. The 8-speed automatic in this generation is bulletproof when it's maintained, and this one clearly has been. Same with the suspension — everything rides and handles like you'd expect from a well-cared-for Tahoe. We wouldn't put it on the lot if there were any red flags in those systems. This is a straight-shooting vehicle, which is why we're confident in it.
